tasks.description: Create migration SQL file at `.migrations_staging/20260419110000_create_moderation_decisions.sql` defining `moderation_decisions` with columns: `id bigserial pk`, `message_id bigint not null`, `channel_id bigint`, `guild_id bigint not null`, `reactor_user_id bigint`, `reactor_name text`, `emoji text not null`, `message_author_id bigint`, `message_author_name text`, `message_content_snippet text`, `classification text not null check (classification in ('bot_auto_restricted','bot_curator_reject','user_self_removal','moderator_cleared_all','moderator_cleared_emoji','message_deleted_cascade'))`, `reason text`, `is_suspicious boolean not null default false`, `removed_at timestamptz not null default now()`. Indexes: `(guild_id, removed_at desc)`, `(message_id)`, `(reactor_user_id, removed_at desc)`. Update `.migrations_staging/README.md` with the new file entry and deploy destination `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/`.

In `src/common/db_handler.py`, add three helpers near `log_reaction_event` (~line 675): (a) `record_moderation_decision(*, message_id, channel_id, guild_id, reactor_user_id, reactor_name, emoji, message_author_id, message_author_name, message_content_snippet, classification, reason=None, is_suspicious=False) -> bool` gated by `_gate_check(guild_id)`, inserting into `moderation_decisions` using the same try/except pattern at lines 685–697; (b) `get_active_reactors(message_id: int, emoji: Optional[str] = None) -> list[dict]` returning `[{'user_id','emoji','guild_id'}]` from `discord_reactions` where `removed_at IS NULL` (filter by emoji when provided); (c) `get_message_snapshot(message_id: int) -> Optional[dict]` returning `{'author_id','author_name','content','channel_id','guild_id'}` from `discord_messages`. Pure additions, no changes to existing helpers.

In `src/features/reacting/reactor_cog.py`: (a) add `self._bot_initiated_removals = {}` in `__init__` (after line 31); (b) add `register_bot_removal(self, message_id, user_id, emoji_str, classification, reason=None)` and `_prune_bot_removals(self, ttl_seconds=60)` methods — register stores `(classification, reason, time.monotonic())` keyed by `(message_id, user_id, emoji_str)` and calls `_prune_bot_removals` on every register to bound memory; (c) refactor `_is_restricted_emoji` (lines 70–87) into `_classify_restricted_emoji(emoji_str) -> Optional[str]` returning `'flag' | 'political' | 'religious' | None`; update the single boolean caller at line 342 to `category = self._classify_restricted_emoji(emoji_str); if category is not None:`; (d) in the restricted-emoji branch (lines 340–371), call `self.register_bot_removal(message.id, user.id, emoji_str, 'bot_auto_restricted', category)` BEFORE `await message.remove_reaction(...)`, then after successful removal call `self.bot.db_handler.record_moderation_decision(...)` with `classification='bot_auto_restricted'`, `reason=category`, `is_suspicious=False`, author/content from the live `message`, and `message_content_snippet=(message.content or '')[:200]`.

In `src/features/reacting/reactor_cog.py` `on_raw_reaction_remove` (lines 410–504), after `user/channel/message/emoji_str` are resolved, consult `self._bot_initiated_removals` with key `(message.id, user.id, emoji_str)`: if present, POP the entry and write exactly one `record_moderation_decision` using the marker's stored `classification` and `reason` (do not also write user_self_removal); if absent, write `classification='user_self_removal'`, `reason=None`, `is_suspicious = emoji_str in {'🤮','👎','😭'}`. When `is_suspicious` is True, fire-and-forget DM `ADMIN_USER_ID` using the `src/common/base_bot.py:176-183` pattern (inline, ≤10 lines, no new config). DM body: reactor mention, emoji, `message.jump_url`, 200-char content snippet. Cascades and moderator clears must never be marked suspicious — suspicion logic lives only in this handler.

In `src/features/reacting/reactor_cog.py` add three new listeners: (a) `@commands.Cog.listener() async def on_raw_reaction_clear(self, payload)` — call `db_handler.get_active_reactors(payload.message_id)`, read snapshot via `db_handler.get_message_snapshot(payload.message_id)`, and write one `record_moderation_decision(classification='moderator_cleared_all', reason=None, is_suspicious=False, ...)` per active reactor (populating `reactor_user_id`, `emoji`, `message_author_*`, `message_content_snippet`, `channel_id`, `guild_id` from the snapshot and active-reactor rows); (b) `@commands.Cog.listener() async def on_raw_reaction_clear_emoji(self, payload)` — same pattern but pass `emoji=str(payload.emoji)` to `get_active_reactors` and use `classification='moderator_cleared_emoji'`; (c) `@commands.Cog.listener() async def on_raw_message_delete(self, payload)` — read the snapshot synchronously at the top of the handler (to avoid races with LoggerCog's soft-delete), enumerate `get_active_reactors(payload.message_id)`, and write one `classification='message_deleted_cascade'` row per reactor. Snapshot failures should be handled gracefully (leave fields null and proceed). No Discord fetch is required in these paths — use DB snapshots only.

In `src/features/curating/curator.py`, immediately BEFORE each of the 4 `await message.remove_reaction('❌', user)` call sites (lines 296, 339, 460, 472), insert:
```
reactor_cog = self.bot.get_cog('ReactorCog')
if reactor_cog:
    reactor_cog.register_bot_removal(message.id, user.id, '❌', 'bot_curator_reject', reason='curator_reject')
```
Use the existing `self.bot` reference — no refactor beyond the 4 insertions. Do NOT write a moderation_decision row directly from curator; the marker is consumed by `on_raw_reaction_remove` (T4) which is the sole writer for this path. Verify line numbers post-edit (earlier insertions will shift later lines; locate each `message.remove_reaction('❌', user)` call fresh before each insertion).

Create `tests/test_moderation_decisions.py` using `conftest.load_module_from_repo` to load `reactor_cog` and `db_handler` with a mocked Supabase client. Monkeypatch `db_handler.record_moderation_decision`, `get_active_reactors`, `get_message_snapshot`, and `bot.fetch_user`. Cover: (1) restricted-emoji add with flag/political/religious → one `bot_auto_restricted` decision with correct reason; marker registered; subsequent `on_raw_reaction_remove` for same key is a no-op; (2) curator `❌` path (simulated): marker registered as `bot_curator_reject`/`reason='curator_reject'`; `on_raw_reaction_remove` writes exactly one decision with that classification/reason; no `user_self_removal` row emitted; (3) plain non-restricted self-removal → `user_self_removal`, `is_suspicious=False`, no admin DM; (4) suspicious self-removal (🤮, 👎, 😭) → `user_self_removal`, `is_suspicious=True`, admin DM attempted via `fetch_user` + `create_dm` + `send` with message jump link, reactor mention, emoji, and 200-char snippet; (5) `on_raw_reaction_clear` with 3 active reactors → 3 `moderator_cleared_all` rows; (6) `on_raw_reaction_clear_emoji` with 2 active reactors for the target emoji and 1 for another emoji → exactly 2 `moderator_cleared_emoji` rows for the target emoji; (7) `on_raw_message_delete` with 2 active reactors → 2 `message_deleted_cascade` rows with author/content populated from `get_message_snapshot`; (8) `_classify_restricted_emoji`: flag codepoints → `'flag'`, 🍉/🔻 → `'political'`, ✝/☪/🕉 → `'religious'`, 👍 → `None`; (9) marker TTL: entries older than 60s are pruned on next `register_bot_removal` call.

Run tests and verify changes. First run `pytest tests/test_moderation_decisions.py -q` to confirm the new module passes. Then run `pytest -q` to catch regressions in reactor/logger/curator tests. If any test fails, read the error, fix the code, and re-run until all pass — do NOT create additional test files beyond T7. Additionally, write a short throwaway script (e.g., `/tmp/smoke_moderation.py`) that instantiates a ReactorCog with mocked bot/db_handler and exercises the suspicious 🤮 self-retract path end-to-end (register no marker, call `on_raw_reaction_remove`, assert exactly one `record_moderation_decision` call with `classification='user_self_removal'`/`is_suspicious=True` and one admin DM send). Run it, confirm the fix behavior, then delete the script. Document in the PR body that `.migrations_staging/20260419110000_create_moderation_decisions.sql` must be copied into `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/` before deploy; until then, inserts error and are swallowed by the try/except.

watch_items: Migration is STAGED only — inserts will fail (and be swallowed) until the SQL file is manually copied to the workspace `supabase/migrations/` repo and deployed. Deploy-order is migration-first.

`_gate_check(guild_id)` silently skips writes for out-of-scope guilds — tests must pass an allowed `guild_id` or monkeypatch the gate, or assertions on `record_moderation_decision` calls will spuriously be zero.

The marker dict `_bot_initiated_removals` is in-memory only — bot restart between a bot-initiated remove and the gateway `on_raw_reaction_remove` event misclassifies the removal as `user_self_removal`. Accepted trade-off; TTL 60s bounds the window anyway.

Marker key is `(message_id, user_id, emoji_str)` — confirm `emoji_str` is normalized the same way at register time (curator + restricted branch) and at consume time (`on_raw_reaction_remove`). Mismatched unicode/custom-emoji strings would leak user_self_removal rows.
Curator line numbers (296, 339, 460, 472) shift after the first insertion. Locate each `message.remove_reaction('❌', user)` freshly before each edit.

`on_raw_message_delete` also has a LoggerCog listener that soft-deletes the row. ReactorCog's listener reads the snapshot synchronously at the top to avoid ordering races, but verify LoggerCog's soft-delete preserves `content`/`author_*` — if it ever nulls them, the snapshot read must run first.

Clear-event payloads (`RawReactionClearEvent`, `RawReactionClearEmojiEvent`) have no actor field. Classifying as `moderator_cleared_*` is safe ONLY because no bot code calls `clear_reactions()`/`clear_reaction()` — if a future bot path adds that, it must pre-register markers to avoid misattribution.

`get_active_reactors` relies on `discord_reactions.removed_at IS NULL` being accurate. If LoggerCog has not yet processed the add event when the clear/cascade fires, the enumeration will miss reactors. Accepted for v1.
Admin DM is fire-and-forget — must not block the event handler or raise unhandled exceptions. Wrap the DM send in its own try/except.
`is_suspicious` is ONLY set on `user_self_removal` of {🤮, 👎, 😭}. Never on cascades, never on moderator clears, never on bot_auto_restricted/bot_curator_reject.

Open minor flag (verifiability-0): Success criterion 12 requires dev-Discord manual smoke (flag reaction, admin reject, 🤮 self-retract, moderator clear-all, message delete) — not automatable; document in PR body.
No new config surfaces, env vars, or feature flags. ADMIN_USER_ID is reused as-is from base_bot.py.
Content snippet is `message.content[:200]` — no sanitization beyond truncation. Truncate at every call site that writes a row.

Debt watch: the task introduces no raw-SQL or RPC calls in `db_handler.py` — keep to the existing fluent-client pattern used by `add_reaction`/`remove_reaction`/`log_reaction_event` to avoid compounding the `db-handler-supabase-infrastructure` debt item.

sense_checks.question: Does the staged migration file exist under `.migrations_staging/` with the timestamp prefix `20260419110000_`, and does the CHECK constraint enumerate EXACTLY the six values {bot_auto_restricted, bot_curator_reject, user_self_removal, moderator_cleared_all, moderator_cleared_emoji, message_deleted_cascade} with no extras and no omissions? Are the three indexes present? Is `.migrations_staging/README.md` updated?
sense_checks.executor_note: Grep the SQL file for each classification string and each index; diff README.md for the new entry.

sense_checks.question: Do `record_moderation_decision`, `get_active_reactors`, and `get_message_snapshot` exist in `src/common/db_handler.py`? Does `record_moderation_decision` call `_gate_check(guild_id)` and use the same try/except pattern as `log_reaction_event` (lines 685–697)? Do the read helpers return the documented dict shapes?
sense_checks.executor_note: Read the three helper bodies; confirm `_gate_check(guild_id)` is the first statement in `record_moderation_decision`. Confirm read helpers use the fluent Supabase client (no raw SQL / RPC) — matches neighbors.

sense_checks.question: Does `ReactorCog.__init__` initialize `self._bot_initiated_removals = {}`? Do `register_bot_removal` and `_prune_bot_removals` exist with the documented signatures? Does `register_bot_removal` invoke pruning on every call? Does `_classify_restricted_emoji` return `'flag' | 'political' | 'religious' | None` and is the old `_is_restricted_emoji` caller at line 342 updated? Does the restricted-emoji branch register a marker BEFORE `remove_reaction` and write ONE `bot_auto_restricted` decision after, with `reason=category` and content truncated to 200 chars?
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirm ordering: `register_bot_removal(...)` is called BEFORE `await message.remove_reaction(...)` so a fast gateway event can consume it. Grep for any remaining references to `_is_restricted_emoji` in src/ — there should be none.

sense_checks.question: In `on_raw_reaction_remove`, when a marker exists for `(message.id, user.id, emoji_str)`, is it popped and used to write exactly ONE row (no duplicate user_self_removal)? When no marker exists, is the row written as `user_self_removal` with `is_suspicious = emoji_str in {🤮, 👎, 😭}`? Is the admin DM fired only on suspicious and wrapped in its own try/except? Does the DM body include reactor mention, emoji, `message.jump_url`, and the 200-char snippet?
sense_checks.executor_note: Trace the control flow to confirm marker-present branch `return`s or `else`s out of the self-removal write. The DM block must not mask write failures — keep DM try/except disjoint from the DB write try/except.

sense_checks.question: Are the three new listeners (`on_raw_reaction_clear`, `on_raw_reaction_clear_emoji`, `on_raw_message_delete`) registered? Does each enumerate `get_active_reactors` and write one row per reactor with the correct classification? Does `on_raw_message_delete` read the snapshot synchronously at the top to avoid races with LoggerCog? Is `is_suspicious=False` on all three paths?
sense_checks.executor_note: Verify `on_raw_message_delete` captures the snapshot BEFORE any `await` that could yield to LoggerCog's listener. If `get_active_reactors` returns empty list, write zero rows (no-op is correct).

sense_checks.question: Are ALL 4 curator `message.remove_reaction('❌', user)` sites (originally at lines 296, 339, 460, 472) preceded by a `register_bot_removal(..., 'bot_curator_reject', reason='curator_reject')` block that safely handles `get_cog('ReactorCog')` returning None? Was no direct `record_moderation_decision` call added in curator (the marker consumer handles the write)?
sense_checks.executor_note: Grep for `remove_reaction('❌'` in curator.py — each match should have a `register_bot_removal` call immediately preceding it. Count must be exactly 4.

sense_checks.question: Does `tests/test_moderation_decisions.py` cover all 9 scenarios listed in the task description? Specifically: restricted flag/political/religious with correct reasons, curator path with no duplicate user_self_removal row, plain self-removal with no DM, each of {🤮, 👎, 😭} firing the DM with correct payload, clear_all enumerating all reactors, clear_emoji filtering to matching reactors only, message_delete cascade with snapshot population, `_classify_restricted_emoji` returns across all branches, and TTL pruning?
sense_checks.executor_note: For the TTL test, monkeypatch `time.monotonic` rather than sleeping. Ensure tests assert on exact kwargs passed to `record_moderation_decision` (classification, reason, is_suspicious) — not just call count.

sense_checks.question: Did `pytest tests/test_moderation_decisions.py -q` pass? Did `pytest -q` pass with no regressions in reactor/logger/curator tests? Did the throwaway smoke script confirm the 🤮 self-retract admin DM path and was it deleted? Does the PR body mention that the migration must be manually copied to the workspace `supabase/migrations/` directory before deploy?
sense_checks.executor_note: Run the baseline `pytest -q --tb=no` BEFORE any edits to capture pre-existing failures; compare to post-change results to isolate regressions. Delete `/tmp/smoke_moderation.py` once it has served its purpose — do not commit it.

meta_commentary: Implementation ordering is strict: migration SQL \u2192 DB helpers \u2192 ReactorCog marker infra + restricted branch \u2192 `on_raw_reaction_remove` consumer \u2192 clear/cascade listeners \u2192 curator markers \u2192 tests \u2192 validate. Steps 3 and 6 both require `register_bot_removal` to exist; T6 depends on T3. Step 4 is the sole writer for the curator path \u2014 do NOT add a `record_moderation_decision` call in curator.py itself. The restricted-emoji branch is the one exception that writes eagerly (it already holds live `user`/`message` objects and can short-circuit downstream event handling); its marker registration exists solely to suppress a duplicate write from the gateway `on_raw_reaction_remove` event. Key gotchas: (1) `_gate_check(guild_id)` will silently skip writes for out-of-scope guilds \u2014 tests must set an allowed guild_id or patch the gate. (2) The marker key `(message_id, user_id, emoji_str)` must use the SAME emoji normalization at register time (curator + restricted branch) and consume time (`on_raw_reaction_remove`); mismatches silently leak user_self_removal rows. (3) `on_raw_message_delete` has a LoggerCog co-listener \u2014 read the DB snapshot synchronously at the top of the ReactorCog handler. (4) Curator line numbers shift after the first insertion; find `message.remove_reaction('\u274c', user)` fresh before each edit. (5) Admin DM must never block or raise into the event loop \u2014 wrap in its own try/except. (6) Suspicion applies ONLY to `user_self_removal` of {\ud83e\udd2e, \ud83d\udc4e, \ud83d\ude2d}; never cascades/clears/bot_auto. (7) Verifiability-0 (manual Discord smoke) is unavoidable \u2014 document in the PR body rather than trying to automate. (8) `.migrations_staging/` is NOT auto-applied; the PR body must explicitly call out the manual copy step to `/Users/peteromalley/Documents/banodoco-workspace/supabase/migrations/`. Debt watch: stick to the fluent Supabase client pattern used by neighbors in `db_handler.py` \u2014 no raw SQL / no RPC, per existing `db-handler-supabase-infrastructure` debt flag.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Stage `moderation_decisions` migration SQL + update staging README

ReactorCog marker infra (`_bot_initiated_removals`, `register_bot_removal`, TTL prune), refactor `_is_restricted_emoji` into `_classify_restricted_emoji`, and wire restricted-emoji branch to register marker + write `bot_auto_restricted` row
ReactorCog `on_raw_reaction_remove`: consume marker (write marker's classification/reason) or classify as `user_self_removal` with suspicion + admin DM for {🤮,👎,😭}
ReactorCog clear + message-delete cascade listeners enumerating active reactors and writing one row per reactor with moderator/cascade classifications
Mark curator ❌ removal call sites (4x) to register `bot_curator_reject` markers before `remove_reaction`
Validate: run targeted + full pytest, smoke the suspicious path, document migration hand-off in PR body

validation.completeness_notes: Every plan step maps to exactly one finalize task. T8 subsumes both the `pytest tests/test_moderation_decisions.py` and full-suite `pytest -q` invocations from the plan's Validation Order, plus the throwaway repro-script requirement from the finalize instructions and the PR-body documentation step. The plan's Step 5.4 (optional `_fetch_channel_and_message` helper factoring) is explicitly called out as 'only if needed' — the new clear/cascade paths use DB snapshots, not Discord fetches, so no helper is required and no separate task is warranted. Manual dev-Discord smoke (plan Validation Order item 3 / success criterion 12) is an info-level criterion that cannot be automated; T8 documents it in the PR body.

baseline_test_note: Baseline pytest run was not executed \u2014 the harness sandbox required approval for `python -m pytest`. The executor should run `python -m pytest -q --tb=no` against the clean working tree at the start of T8 (before applying any edits that could mask regressions) to capture the true baseline, then re-run after changes to diff for new failures. If pre-existing failures surface unrelated to reacting/logging/curator/db_handler, treat them as baseline noise and note in PR body.
